text: The flexible sigmoidoscopy and cystoscopy reveal a fistula connecting the colorectal anastomosis to the distal ureteral stump, which had been previously transected.
The video shows the port configuration setup for robotic surgery, detailing the arrangement and placement of ports on the patient's abdomen.
The transanal port is visibly placed while the patient is positioned in the prone jackknife position, facilitating access for the procedure.
A rigid proctoscope is used to identify the fistula opening located at seventeen centimeters from the anal verge.
The robotic surgical system is docked parallel to the operating room table, ready for the procedure.
The surgeon identifies the lesion and proceeds to over sew it using a 2-0 absorbable self-locking suture, securing the tissue in place.
The surgeon performs running suturing to completely over-sew the fistula opening, using a continuous technique to close the defect.
The patient is positioned in lithotomy, and a cystoscopy is performed, providing a view of the bladder and urethra.
